description: A structured AI Product Leader learning book for understanding modern AI systems, prompting, token economics, RAG, agents, MCP, production AI infrastructure, and applied AI product strategy.
sourceNote: Normalized from published site content. Replace by re-running syllabus import when AI_Product_Leader_Syllabus.xlsx (Master Syllabus) is placed at repo root.

modules.title: Foundations of Modern AI Systems
modules.pmFocus: Every token costs money. Understanding tokenization, context windows, and model tiers lets you set product constraints, write better specs, and own AI feature unit economics.
modules.deliverable: Model selection memo — use case, quality requirements, cost estimate at scale, chosen model + rationale, risks
learningOutcomes: Estimate monthly inference cost for a feature
Choose the right model tier for a use case
Have a credible LLM conversation with engineers

chapters.title: Understanding Attention Intuition from "The Illustrated Transformer"
Tokenization and Context Windows for Product Leaders
Temperature, Top-p and Sampling — Why Same Prompt Gives Different Outputs
Choosing Between GPT, Claude, Gemini and Llama
Hallucinations, Knowledge Cutoffs and Model Limitations
Benchmarks, Cost per Token and Model Tier Tradeoffs

modules.title: Prompt Engineering — Techniques & Structure
modules.pmFocus: Prompt engineering is the new acceptance criteria. A well-structured prompt defines "done" for the AI as clearly as a user story does for an engineer.
modules.deliverable: 3 production-quality system prompts for features in your product + an eval rubric for each

modules.title: Token-Efficient Prompting
modules.pmFocus: At 1M calls/day, a 3x reduction in prompt tokens can be the difference between a profitable and unprofitable AI feature. Token efficiency is a product economics lever.
modules.deliverable: Token efficiency audit — compress 3 prompts by ≥50%, validate quality parity, document savings at scale

modules.title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)
modules.pmFocus: RAG is the most common production pattern for grounding LLMs in company knowledge. Retrieval precision directly reduces context tokens.
modules.deliverable: RAG pipeline spec — chunking strategy, embedding model, vector DB, retrieval approach, eval plan, freshness strategy

modules.title: AI Agents & Agentic Systems
modules.pmFocus: Agents introduce compounding failure modes and latency. The PM's job is defining HITL boundaries and what "done" looks like for an agent task.
modules.deliverable: Agentic feature PRD — scope, tool list, HITL checkpoints, blast radius controls, observability, token cost per run

modules.title: Model Context Protocol (MCP)
modules.pmFocus: MCP is becoming the USB-C of AI tool connectivity. Understanding when to expose your product via MCP is a platform strategy question.
modules.deliverable: MCP build-vs-skip decision memo — who benefits, tools to expose, security requirements

modules.title: AI Infrastructure & the Production Stack
modules.pmFocus: Observability tools let you see token spend by feature, by user segment, and by prompt version. This is your cost-of-goods dashboard for AI.
modules.deliverable: AI observability spec — metrics, dashboards, alerts, cost-per-feature breakdown

modules.title: AI Product Strategy & Applied Practice
modules.pmFocus: The capstone synthesizes everything. Pick a real problem and design the full AI system — including token budget, caching strategy, eval plan, guardrails, and product decisions.
modules.deliverable: Capstone — full agentic feature design with PRD, token budget, eval strategy, guardrails, HITL design, unit economics
